greeting: Noticing any Supply Chain Disruptions? Hop In,

messages.label: Actions
messages.content:
My entire cotton warehouse stock is lost. I must deliver 30 units of clothing in 7 days. Production takes 3 days for the whole batch, leaving me exactly 4 days to procure 30 units of cotton.

Based on this 4-day sourcing window, what specific businesses, factory types, or emergency distributors do I need to reach out to right now?

messages.label: Simulation Ran
messages.summary:
Simulation Complete:
Evaluated emergency procurement routes for 30 units of cotton within a strict 96-hour window.

options.title: Option 1 : Optimal (Balanced)
points: Action: Dispatch micro-orders to established Northern nodes (Blue Routes). Delivery in 48 hours.
Risk Type: [Logistical] Minor local transit variables, but leaves a comfortable 2-day buffer for production.

options.title: Option 2 : Fast & High Risk
points: Action: Immediate spot-market buyout from all unvetted local brokers. Delivery in <24 hours.
Risk Type: [Financial & Quality] Severe price markups and untested material standards.
